# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains benchmark-construction details, task-scale metrics, formal evaluation definitions, comparative performance results for coding agents, trajectory-level failure analysis, and stated limitations for the SWE-EVO benchmark.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: claim-support
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, abstract/introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
SWE-EVO contains 48 software-evolution tasks drawn from 7 mature open-source Python projects.

## Source Excerpt

"Constructed from release notes of seven mature open-source Python projects, SWE-EVO comprises 48 tasks" and later "In total, SWE-EVO comprises 48 such evolution tasks across 7 open repositories."

## Note

The source presents this as the benchmark’s final composition, not as a projected target.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: metric
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The mean SWE-EVO gold patch edits 610.5 lines across 20.9 files and 51.0 functions.

## Source Excerpt

Table 1 reports: "Gold Patch # Lines edited 610.5", "# Files edited 20.9", and "# Func. edited 51.0".

## Note

These are micro-averages across task instances rather than per-repository averages.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: metric
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
SWE-EVO task test suites average 81.4 FAIL_TO_PASS tests and 874.0 total tests per instance.

## Source Excerpt

Table 1 lists under Tests: "# Fail to Pass 81.4" and "# Total 874.0".

## Note

The source frames these test counts as part of the benchmark’s verification burden.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: method
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Section 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
SWE-EVO retains only candidates with at least one FAIL_TO_PASS test and discards candidates with installation or runtime errors.

## Source Excerpt

"We retain only instances that exhibit at least one FAIL_TO_PASS test ... ensuring a measurable behavioral change attributable to the required evolution. We additionally discard candidates that trigger installation or runtime errors under the benchmark environment."

## Note

This describes an execution-based filtering rule used during dataset construction.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: method
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Section 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In SWE-EVO, the model receives a release-note item plus the complete pre-release codebase and must output a patch implementing the change.

## Source Excerpt

"A model is provided with (i) a release-note item that specifies the intended change ... and (ii) a complete codebase at the pre-release commit. The model must produce edits to the codebase that implement the described change."

## Note

The source distinguishes the generated "model patch" from the ground-truth "gold patch."
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Section 3.2 Resolved Rate
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Resolved Rate counts an instance as solved only if all FAIL_TO_PASS and PASS_TO_PASS tests pass under the model patch.

## Source Excerpt

"The Resolved Rate of an instance is equal 1 if all tests in both FAIL_TO_PASS and PASS_TO_PASS are PASSED; otherwise, it is 0."

## Note

This is a strict binary success criterion.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Section 3.2 Fix Rate
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Fix Rate gives partial credit for fixed FAIL_TO_PASS tests, but assigns the entire instance a score of 0 if any PASS_TO_PASS test regresses.

## Source Excerpt

"The Fix Rate measures progress by computing what fraction of FAIL_TO_PASS tests the model successfully fixes. Crucially, it also enforces a regression constraint: if the model’s patch breaks any PASS_TO_PASS test ... the entire instance receives a score of 0."

## Note

This metric preserves a no-regression condition while exposing partial progress that the binary Resolved Rate hides.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: result
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: supports
---
With release-note plus PR/issue context, gpt-5-08-07 resolved 18.75% of SWE-EVO tasks in OpenHands and 20.83% in SWE-agent, versus 65.00% on SWE-bench Verified.

## Source Excerpt

Table 2 reports for "gpt-5-08-07 (medium reasoning)": "18.75" resolved for OpenHands, "20.83" resolved for SWE-agent, and "65.00" on SWE-bench Verified.

## Note

The source uses this comparison to show a performance gap between SWE-EVO and SWE-bench Verified.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: result
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Table 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In the release-note-only setting, gpt-5-08-07 resolved 14.58% of SWE-EVO tasks in OpenHands and 16.67% in SWE-agent.

## Source Excerpt

Table 3 lists for "gpt-5-08-07 (medium reasoning)": "% Resolved 14.58" under OpenHands and "% Resolved 16.67" under SWE-agent.

## Note

These results are lower than the same model’s scores in the release-note-plus-PR/issue setting.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: result
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Adding PR/issue context improved SWE-EVO results only modestly, according to the authors.

## Source Excerpt

"Finally, providing PR/issue context yields measurable but modest gains, suggesting that even with detailed and fully specified context, agents still struggle to reconstruct the correct release-note-aligned implementation change."

## Note

This is an author interpretation of the release-note-only versus context-augmented comparison rather than a single tabulated metric.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: result
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Section 4.3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
For gpt-5, more than 60% of unresolved SWE-EVO trajectories were labeled as instruction-following failures.

## Source Excerpt

"Among the GPT-series, the strongest model (gpt-5) almost never fails due to syntax or tool errors; instead, its unresolved cases are dominated by Instruction Following mistakes (over 60%)."

## Note

This result comes from an LLM-as-a-judge failure-mode analysis of unresolved SWE-agent trajectories.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: metric
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Section 4.3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Instances solved less often were associated with more pull requests, with mean PR counts of 14.84, 6.71, 3.57, and 1.67 across difficulty groups from hardest to easiest.

## Source Excerpt

"Figure 6(a) shows a clear monotonic trend ... The average PR counts corresponding are: 14.84, 6.71, 3.57, and 1.67 across the four groups."

## Note

The source presents PR count as a practical proxy for SWE-EVO instance difficulty.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: incident
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Limitations
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The authors state that SWE-EVO currently covers only Python projects, uses release notes as specifications, and has 48 instances, which they say limits statistical power for fine-grained comparisons.

## Source Excerpt

"SWE-EVO currently covers only Python projects and relies on release notes as specifications, which may not capture all evolution scenarios ... The 48-instance scale, while ensuring quality, limits statistical power for fine-grained comparisons."

## Note

This is an explicit limitation statement that bounds how broadly the benchmark results should be generalized.
---

role: Extractor

```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1

evidence_id: evidence-13

contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The authors state that SWE-EVO currently covers only Python projects, uses release notes as specifications, and has 48 instances, which they say limits statistical power for fine-grained comparisons.

## Source Excerpt

"SWE-EVO currently covers only Python projects and relies on release notes as specifications, which may not capture all evolution scenarios ... The 48-instance scale, while ensuring quality, limits statistical power for fine-grained comparisons."

## Note

This is an explicit limitation statement that bounds how broadly the benchmark results should be generalized.

# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains benchmark-construction details, task-scale metrics, formal evaluation definitions, comparative performance results for coding agents, trajectory-level failure analysis, and stated limitations for the SWE-EVO benchmark.

## Evidence Files

---

contradiction_relevance: none
---
SWE-EVO contains 48 software-evolution tasks drawn from 7 mature open-source Python projects.

## Source Excerpt

"Constructed from release notes of seven mature open-source Python projects, SWE-EVO comprises 48 tasks" and later "In total, SWE-EVO comprises 48 such evolution tasks across 7 open repositories."

## Note

The source presents this as the benchmark’s final composition, not as a projected target.
```

## Note

The source frames these test counts as part of the benchmark’s verification burden.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: method
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Section 3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
SWE-EVO retains only candidates with at least one FAIL_TO_PASS test and discards candidates with installation or runtime errors.

## Source Excerpt

"We retain only instances that exhibit at least one FAIL_TO_PASS test ... ensuring a measurable behavioral change attributable to the required evolution. We additionally discard candidates that trigger installation or runtime errors under the benchmark environment."

## Note

This describes an execution-based filtering rule used during dataset construction.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: method
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Section 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In SWE-EVO, the model receives a release-note item plus the complete pre-release codebase and must output a patch implementing the change.

## Source Excerpt

"A model is provided with (i) a release-note item that specifies the intended change ... and (ii) a complete codebase at the pre-release commit. The model must produce edits to the codebase that implement the described change."

## Note

The source distinguishes the generated "model patch" from the ground-truth "gold patch."
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Section 3.2 Resolved Rate
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Resolved Rate counts an instance as solved only if all FAIL_TO_PASS and PASS_TO_PASS tests pass under the model patch.

## Source Excerpt

"The Resolved Rate of an instance is equal 1 if all tests in both FAIL_TO_PASS and PASS_TO_PASS are PASSED; otherwise, it is 0."

## Note

This is a strict binary success criterion.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-004
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Thai et al., SWE-EVO, Section 3.2 Fix Rate
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2512.18470v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Fix Rate gives partial credit for fixed FAIL_TO_PASS tests, but assigns the entire instance a score of 0 if any PASS_TO_PASS test regresses.

## Source Excerpt

"The Fix Rate measures progress by computing what fraction of FAIL_TO_PASS tests the model successfully fixes. Crucially, it also enforces a regression constraint: if the model’s patch breaks any PASS_TO_PASS test ... the entire instance receives a score of 0."

## Note

This metric preserves a no-regression condition while exposing partial progress that the binary Resolved Rate hides.
```
